mcqs.question: What is the index of the first element in a C language array?
mcqs.explanation: In C, C++, Java, and most programming languages, arrays use zero-based indexing, meaning the first element is accessed using index 0.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of accessing any element in an array by its index?
mcqs.explanation: Array elements are stored in contiguous memory, so any element can be accessed directly using its index in constant time O(1).

mcqs.question: Which operation has O(n) time complexity in arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Searching by value requires traversing the array in the worst case, giving O(n) time complexity. Access by index is O(1).

mcqs.question: In a 3×4 matrix stored in row-major order, what is the index of element arr[2][3]?
mcqs.explanation: In row-major: index = (i × number_of_columns) + j = (2 × 4) + 3 = 8 + 3 = 11.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of static arrays?

options.text: Fixed size cannot be changed at runtime

mcqs.explanation: Static arrays have a fixed size declared at compile time and cannot be resized during runtime, leading to either memory wastage or overflow.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Arrays by default have fixed size. Dynamic sizing requires special data structures like dynamic arrays (ArrayList, vectors) which internally manage resizing.

mcqs.question: In a sparse matrix with most zero elements, what is the efficient way to store it?

options.text: Compressed sparse row (CSR) or coordinate list (COO)

mcqs.explanation: Sparse matrices are efficiently stored using special formats like CSR, CSC, or COO that store only non-zero elements with their positions.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of storing an array of n integers?
mcqs.explanation: Space complexity is O(n) because we need n memory locations to store n elements.

mcqs.question: Which memory allocation method is used for dynamic arrays?

options.text: Heap allocation using malloc/calloc (C) or new (C++)

mcqs.explanation: Dynamic arrays are allocated on the heap using functions like malloc/calloc in C or new operator in C++, allowing runtime size determination.

mcqs.question: What happens when you insert an element at the beginning of a full array?

options.text: All elements shift right and array may resize if dynamic

mcqs.explanation: In a dynamic array, inserting at the beginning requires shifting all elements right. If the array is full, it resizes (typically doubles) then inserts.

mcqs.question: What is a jagged array?

options.text: An array with all zero elements
A 2D array where each row can have different number of columns
An array sorted in descending order

mcqs.explanation: A jagged array (array of arrays) allows each inner array to have different lengths, unlike a rectangular 2D array.

mcqs.question: For a polynomial 3x² + 2x + 5, what would be stored at index 2 in its array representation?
mcqs.explanation: In array representation of polynomials, the index represents the exponent and the value represents the coefficient. So index 2 (x²) stores 3.

mcqs.question: Which of these is a disadvantage of contiguous memory allocation in arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Contiguous allocation can cause memory fragmentation when resizing requires allocating a new larger block and copying all elements.

mcqs.question: What is the base address of an array?
mcqs.explanation: The base address is the memory address of the first element (index 0) of the array.

mcqs.question: In a 2D array declared as arr[3][4] in C, how many elements can it store?
mcqs.explanation: A 2D array with dimensions [3][4] can store 3 × 4 = 12 elements (3 rows and 4 columns).

mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of searching an element in an unsorted array?
mcqs.explanation: In an unsorted array, we must check each element in the worst case, giving O(n) linear search time complexity.

mcqs.question: Which programming language feature provides built-in dynamic arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Python lists and Java ArrayList are dynamic array implementations that automatically resize, unlike static arrays in C.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of deleting an element from the end of a dynamic array?
mcqs.explanation: Deleting from the end of a dynamic array is O(1) amortized because no shifting of elements is required.

mcqs.question: How is a 3D array typically stored in memory?

options.text: As a linear sequence following row-major or column-major order

mcqs.explanation: Even multidimensional arrays are stored as a single contiguous block in memory, following either row-major or column-major order.

mcqs.question: Which of these operations is NOT efficient in a static array?
mcqs.explanation: Static arrays cannot be resized efficiently. Resizing requires creating a new larger array and copying all elements, which is O(n).
